rules: RULE-01: The game starts at scene 'start' with empty clues/beats/logs. Each choice in input.choices is applied in order, up to MAX_STEPS.

RULE-02: A choice valid for the current scene transitions to its target scene and logs '선택:<choiceId>'. An invalid choice (not offered by the current scene) is logged '무시:<choiceId>' and changes nothing else.
RULE-03: When the chosen option carries a clue not yet collected, append it to clues and log '단서:<CLUE>'. Clues are unique (collecting an owned clue is a no-op).
RULE-04: After a clue is collected, re-evaluate beats. Beat DEDUCTION fires exactly once when all three core clues (WOUND, LETTER, PRINT) are held; firing logs 'BEAT:DEDUCTION'.
RULE-05: The 'accuse' choice (verdict) ends the game: scene becomes end_solved (ending TRUTH) if all three core clues are held, otherwise end_wrong (ending COLD_CASE).

RULE-06: Reaching any scene that declares an ending sets state.ending and isGameOver=true; remaining choices are not processed. The 'refuse' choice leads to end_refused (ending WALKED_AWAY); 'walk' at confront leads to end_wrong (COLD_CASE).

RULE-07 (THIS CARD): FALSE_ALIBI. Add a butler path: the crime_scene offers a new choice 'question_butler' -> scene 'butler_room', which offers 'confront_alibi' (clue ALIBI, returns to crime_scene) and 'leave_butler' (returns to crime_scene). Beat FALSE_ALIBI fires exactly once when both ALIBI and LETTER are held (the torn letter exposes the alibi as false); firing logs 'BEAT:FALSE_ALIBI'. ALIBI is NOT a core clue and does not affect the DEDUCTION/verdict conditions. Any play that never questions the butler produces byte-for-byte identical output to before.

files.description: Scene graph data (B layer). THIS card adds the 'question_butler' choice to crime_scene and the new 'butler_room' scene (clue ALIBI). All existing scenes/choices kept intact.
Beat-firing logic (A layer). THIS card adds the FALSE_ALIBI beat (ALIBI && LETTER) alongside the existing DEDUCTION beat. Keep fireBeats(clues, firedBeats) signature.
